title: Working at MDA
text:
Many residents — whether families or singles — agree that living in the Tennessee Valley is exciting because there are so many attractions, and they’re all easily accessible. You can see a Broadway play(external link opens in new window), listen to a symphony(external link opens in new window), visit an art museum(external link opens in new window) and play a championship golf course(external link opens in new window) without being concerned about traffic and parking.
Visitors who want to be “astronauts for a day” can sample astronaut-training activities at the sprawling U.S. Space and Rocket Center.(external link opens in new window) Downtown Huntsville’s variety of museums and attractions(external link opens in new window) and its outstanding symphony orchestra offer rich cultural opportunities involving the arts. Nearby, visit Limestone County’s annual Renaissance Festival, Cullman’s Oktoberfest(external link opens in new window), or Hartselle’s nationally recognized Depot Days(external link opens in new window) festival.
The Tennessee Valley also delivers a sportsman’s paradise — fishing(external link opens in new window), hunting, boating(external link opens in new window), water skiing(external link opens in new window), picnicking(external link opens in new window), hiking(external link opens in new window), biking(external link opens in new window) and more. The legendary Robert Trent Jones Golf Trail,(external link opens in new window) which encompasses 26 courses in 11 cities in Alabama, begins here at the 54-hole Hampton Cove Golf Course(external link opens in new window).
Those who enjoy top-flight recreation will appreciate a hiking expedition along one of the area’s numerous park trails or lakes, a kayak trip down a river, fishing in a lake, or another in the wide range of area activities. There also is a Top Golf and a variety of movie theaters, an outdoor mall (Bridge Street Town Center), and ever-growing areas such as downtown, Providence, and MidCity.
Spectator sports are also strong. Huntsville also boasts a minor league professional hockey team, the Havoc, as well as a Double-A baseball team, the Rocket City Trash Pandas, in nearby Madison. Fall Saturdays mean football season in Alabama as locals trek to the University of Alabama, Auburn, Tennessee, Alabama A&M, UAB, North Alabama, and other top schools to catch the action. The Havoc regularly draw thousands of hockey fans to Huntsville’s downtown Von Braun Center during the fall and winter, and the Trash Pandas do the same at their brand new ballpark just outside of Redstone Arsenal. You’re only a short drive from professional teams such as the Nashville Predators, Tennessee Titans, and the Atlanta Braves, Falcons, and Hawks as well.
Fine arts are also well represented by dance, theater and museum events throughout the year.
Nashville's Music City; Atlanta's vibrant urban pace; Birmingham's blossoming bustle; Memphis with all its music and food; and Montgomery, the birthplace of the civil rights movement and Alabama's capital city, are all within easy driving distance.
Recreational destinations include the Great Smoky Mountains National Park, Lake Ocoee, and the Cherokee National Forest.
